title: triainolepis africana subsp. africana
intro: Triainolepis africana subsp. africana, also known as the African wild tea, is a shrub native to South Africa, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Triainolepis africana subsp. africana include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating digestive issues. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or it can be used in various other herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions.
